question_title: Which range will the CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) close level fall in on April 30, 2026?
summary: VIX is at 29.49 (spiked 24% on March 6) during an active US/Israel war on Iran that began February 28, 2026. Iran's Supreme Leader was killed, and the Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed, disrupting 20% of global oil supply (Brent crude at $92+). Both sides reject negotiations. The forecast must weigh VIX's powerful mean-reversion tendency (verified: Russia-Ukraine peaked ~37 → ~21 in 40 days; tariff shock peaked 52 → ~22 in 40 days) against the unprecedented Hormuz closure creating a structural oil/economic shock with no historical analogue. The 25+ and 20-25 ranges dominate: if the war persists through April with Hormuz disrupted, VIX stays above 25; if markets adapt as they did during Russia-Ukraine, VIX reverts to 20-25. Quick resolution (15-20) requires concrete de-escalation within weeks. Below 15 is near-impossible during active conflict.

factors.description: Active US/Israel war on Iran (started Feb 28, 2026) with regime-change objectives. Supreme Leader killed. War in Day 7, both sides publicly rejecting negotiations/ceasefire.

Strait of Hormuz effectively closed - 20% of global daily oil supply disrupted. Brent crude at $92+. This is UNPRECEDENTED - no prior VIX analogue involved complete closure of a critical oil chokepoint, meaning this structural shock pushes VIX above what historical mean-reversion analogues would suggest.

VIX mean-reversion verified with data: Russia-Ukraine peaked ~37, reverted to ~21 in 40 trading days. April 2025 tariff shock peaked 52, reverted to ~22 in 40 days. Current VIX of 29.49 is lower than both peaks, supporting reversion toward 20-23 range - but these analogues lacked the Hormuz closure factor.

Oil futures in steep backwardation (May $92 to Aug $80), suggesting markets expect partial supply normalization. Trump offering naval escorts. But Aug futures at $80 still 14% above pre-crisis levels, showing persistent impact.

Trump stated war could last '4-5 weeks' - ending early-to-mid April, leaving limited time for VIX to mean-revert before April 30. Back-channel talks (Iran CIA outreach) suggest some diplomatic progress possible.

Risk of escalation: ground invasion, broader regional war (Hezbollah active), strikes on Gulf allies. Trump demanded 'unconditional surrender' which Iran rejected. War Day 7 is too early for resolution expectations.
VIX below 15 requires full crisis resolution plus strong market rally. Near-impossible during active war with global oil supply disruption.

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option whose range contains the VIX close value for April 30, 2026 as shown in FRED series VIXCLS using https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/VIXCLS
fine_print: If April 30, 2026 has no VIXCLS observation (market holiday), use the most recent prior trading day’s observation. Use the value shown on FRED (do not re-compute). Candidate Sources: CBOE VIX historical data (backup) https://www.cboe.com/tradable_products/vix/vix_historical_data/ | Stooq VIX historical data (backup) https://stooq.com/q/d/l/?s=%5Evix
